title: httpd container started with podman not able to serve request on localhost
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

When starting any container (in my case httpd) and map a host port and try to send a request to localhost on mapped port, it is not working.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```bash
# podman run -d -p 8000:80 httpd:latest
5cde892e79febf5402d2ca5c22ad2a4ea113eb31f4fc6a908e2bd7bd231c9483

# podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND           CREATED         STATUS             PORTS                 NAMES
5cde892e79fe  docker.io/library/httpd:latest  httpd-foreground  4 seconds ago   Up 3 seconds ago   0.0.0.0:8000->80/tcp  gracious_roentgen

# netstat -ntpl | grep 8000
tcp        2      0 0.0.0.0:8000            0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      116109/conmon    
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```bash
# curl -v localhost:8000 -m 10
* Rebuilt URL to: localhost:8000/
*   Trying ::1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* connect to ::1 port 8000 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8000 (#0)
> GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8000
> User-Agent: curl/7.61.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Operation timed out after 10001 milliseconds with 0 bytes received
* Closing connection 0
curl: (28) Operation timed out after 10001 milliseconds with 0 bytes received
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Should able to get the request.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```bash
# podman version
Version:            1.4.2-stable2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.8
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
# podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.8
  podman version: 1.4.2-stable2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.2-5.el8.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhcos"'
    version: "4.2"
  MemFree: 789745664
  MemTotal: 10423308288
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-61.rc8.rhaos4.2.git3cbe540.el8.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: crc-rqjqw-master-0
  kernel: 4.18.0-80.11.1.el8_0.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 3h 10m 42.07s (Approximately 0.12 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 160
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 64
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```bash
# rpm -aq podman
podman-1.4.2-5.el8.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is in the coreos image which is run using installer https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp-dev-preview/4.2.0-0.nightly-2019-09-19-153821/ from here on libvirt provider.

I test same on the Fedora 30 with podman-1.5.1 and didn't see this issue :(

comments.body: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1747429 is pretty much what we are experiencing.
This sounds to me like the issue the firewall plugin in 1.5.0 and higher was supposed to solve.
`ls -al /etc/cni/net.d/*` is a good starting point, to verify if it's the same bug.

@mheon  let me know if you need the content of those files.

```
# ls -al /etc/cni/net.d/*
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 294 Sep 17 09:02 /etc/cni/net.d/100-crio-bridge.conf
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root  54 Sep 17 09:02 /etc/cni/net.d/200-loopback.conf
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 483 Sep 17 09:02 /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist
```
100-crio-bridge.conf exists, so I think we're definitely seeing 1747429 here.
@mheon right, I am also going through all the comments on the issue and it is same unfortunately :(
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
I'm closing this as it's resolved on upstream Podman releases
